title: A Seminar on Tax Awareness for Women Entrepreneurs in Rustaq
description: As part of the Tax Authority’s ongoing efforts to build bridges of communication with the local community and simplify financial and legal concepts, the Tax Authority Department in Al Batinah South Governorate organized an awareness seminar titled The seminar was presented by Payment Analyst Nihal Al Qarni and Rahil Al Farsi. The session highlighted the rights and obligations that ensure women entrepreneurs' tax compliance, thereby supporting the growth and stability of their business ventures. Furthermore, the seminar detailed the mechanisms for calculating Income Tax and the categories subject to it, as well as how to manage Value Added Tax (VAT) in commercial activities to ensure the accuracy of accounting records. The seminar also addressed Excise Tax, the excise goods, and the health and economic dimensions that the Sultanate of Oman aims to achieve through its implementation.

title: General Tax Authority Introduces Excise Tax Warehouse Licensing Service
description: As part of its efforts to enhance the implementation of excise tax and streamline related procedures, the General Tax Authority intends to launch the excise tax warehouse licensing service. This service enables the licensing premises designated for the production, processing, possession, storage, or receipt of excise goods, whether locally produced or imported, under the tax suspension regime. This regime refers to the deferral of excise tax liability on such goods while they remain within a licensed tax warehouse, in accordance with the provisions of the Excise Tax Law and its Executive Regulations. The excise tax warehouse licensing service will be available starting from 1 April 2026 to companies producing excise goods, with plans to expand the scope of the service at a later stage to include other eligible entities. The Authority explained that the tax warehouse regime provides a flexible framework for managing excise tax, allowing the suspension of tax payment until the goods are released into the local market. It also enables the import and storage of excise goods and raw materials under a tax suspension status, thereby enhancing taxpayers’ cash flow, supporting production needs, and ensuring supply chain stability. In addition, it contributes to improved inventory management and more efficient operational planning. The tax warehouse service is considered a key enabler for supporting the business environment, as it simplifies administrative procedures related to the refund of excise tax on the import of raw materials, such as concentrates, as well as in cases involving the export of excise goods. The implementation of this service eliminates the need to submit refund applications in such cases, thereby reducing the administrative burden on registered taxpayers. The General Tax Authority affirmed that the launch of this service reflects its role of being an active partner in supporting the national economy and fostering an attractive business environment.

title: The Enhancement of Tax Awareness for Entrepreneurs
description: As part of institutional partnership to support the Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) sector, the Tax Authority department in Al Batinah South Governorate conducted a workshop titled "Tax Basics for Entrepreneurs." The session targeted participants in the "Entrepreneur Readiness Program – Establishment Phase" organized by the (SMEs) Development Authority in the governorate. Asaad Al Balushi, Senior Tax Awareness Analyst, presented a working paper introducing the tax system in the Sultanate of Oman and its recent developments. He clarified tax laws, obligations, and rights to instill a solid tax culture among entrepreneurs at the beginning of their professional careers. The working paper also reviewed aspects of the tax system, including: Principles of Income Tax and its handling mechanisms, applications of Value Added Tax (VAT) and goods subject to Excise Tax. Furthermore, the workshop raised the risks of tax evasion under the slogan "No to Tax Evasion," emphasizing the importance of voluntary compliance to strengthen the national economy.

title: Tax Awareness Workshop at Al Dhahirah Chamber
description: The Tax Authority, in collaboration with the Oman Chamber of Commerce and Industry (OCCI) branch in Al Dhahirah Governorate, organized a workshop on tax awareness and the promotion of voluntary compliance. The event was held for entrepreneurs and owners of small and medium enterprises (SMEs) across the Wilayats of Al Dhahirah Governorate. Ahmed Al Hinai, Senior Tax Awareness Analyst at the Tax Authority, showed a presentation on the Value Added Tax (VAT) law. He defined the tax imposed on all supplies of goods and services in the Sultanate of Oman, explaining the VAT mechanism, types of supplies, tax rates, and tax-exempt supplies. He also presented practical examples regarding the reverse charge mechanism and the tax due date. Later, Mazen Al Abri, a Tax Auditor at the Tax Authority, presented a working paper on the VAT registration mechanism. He clarified who is required to register in VAT and demonstrated the components of annual revenues, which include the value of supplies for registration purposes and tax groups. Additionally, Maryam Al Manthari, a Tax Auditor at the Tax Authority, presented a working paper on the mechanism of filing tax returns. In conclusion, all inquiries and questions from the attendees were addressed, covering everything related to taxes, the challenges entrepreneurs face in applying VAT and Income Tax, and how to pay taxes electronically.
